title: Lessingia tenuis
intro: Lessingia tenuis, also known as Slender Sea Lavender or Slender Coyote Plant, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Baja California. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits for wound healing and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Lessingia tenuis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed to aid in the management of minor skin irritations and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Lessingia tenuis is classified as a member of the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family comprises of over 23,000 species, many of which are found in temperate and subtropical regions around the world. Lessingia tenuis is further classified as a genus within the tribe Madinae, which is a group of daisy-like plants found in North and Central America. The specific classification of Lessingia tenuis is as follows: Lessingia tenuis is a species within the family Asteraceae, genus Lessingia, and tribe Madinae. The exact classification of Lessingia tenuis can be represented in a taxonomic hierarchy as: Lessingia tenuis, Lessingia, Madinae, Asteraceae.
